---
title: "Anthony Zorney"
image: photos/profile.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: rounded
  image-width: 15em
  links:
    - text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/anthony-zorney/
    - text: Email
      href: mailto:zorney.anthony@gmail.com
    - text: Instagram
      href: https://www.instagram.com/az_wildlife_photography
---

*Environmental biologist and PSM candidate seeking roles in ecological restoration, environmental monitoring, and permitting and compliance.*

**About me**

I am an environmental biologist with applied field experience in stream habitat assessment, freshwater ecology, and species surveys, currently completing a Professional Science Master's degree in Ecosystem Science and Sustainability at Colorado State University. My graduate training spans watershed modeling, GIS-based water resources analysis, life cycle assessment, and NEPA-based environmental impact analysis, building on a foundation of field and laboratory work that includes electrofishing surveys, water quality monitoring, and protected species assessments conducted in collaboration with the Michigan Department of Natural Resources.

My research background includes fish and salamander population studies, freshwater quality analysis, and molecular conservation work, with findings contributing to a peer-reviewed publication and agency-facing management reports. I am comfortable designing and executing field surveys, analyzing ecological data in R, and translating results into technical documentation for conservation and management audiences.

I am seeking roles in environmental consulting, ecological restoration and monitoring, and environmental permitting and compliance where I can apply both my field experience and my applied graduate training to meaningful environmental outcomes.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
